title: Used and Re-refined Oils Group - Canada.ca
content:
## Used and Re-refined Oils Group
- Used and Re-refined Oils Group – information sheet
- Consultation: Proposed regulations for polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) in sealant products
These substances were identified for action under the Chemicals Management Plan (CMP). This screening assessment focuses on 8 of 9 substances  referred to collectively under the  CMP as the Used and Re-refined Oils Group.
- Summary of publications
Public comments on the draft screening assessment
Final screening assessment
Conclusion on section 64 criteria
Follow-up activities
Used and Re-refined Oils
Lubricant oils, used, residues
None planned at this time
Lubricant oils, used, vacuum distd.
C 12-25 used distillate
Hydrocarbons, C 12-25 , dehydrated used lubricating oil distillates
C 5-18 used distillate
Lubricant oils, used, distd., C 5-18 fraction
Lubricant oils, used, distd., light oil
Hydrotreated residue
Lubricating oils (petroleum), hydrotreated, used, distd., residues
Lubricating oils, refined used
Clay-treated used oil
Lubricating oils, used, vacuum distd., clay-treated
Publication of the final screening assessment. The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 154, No. 52 .
Publication and start of 60-day public comment period on the draft screening assessment. The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 153, No. 14 .
One other substance in this group, lubricant oils, used (CAS RN 70514-12-4), also referred to as used oil, was found to meet the broad classification of used crankcase oils, and is considered to have been addressed through the Priority Substances List Assessment Report of Waste/Used Crankcase Oil in 1994 and the follow-up report in 2005. As such, this substance will not be subject to further risk assessment work at this time under the CMP given previous regulatory activities.
